gazette: Official Gazette dated 13 April 2023 and numbered 32162
title: Duty exemption will be granted for imported goods for the repair of damages in the areas affected by the Kahramanmaraş earthquake
bodyHtml:
Exemption will be granted for imported goods for the repair of damages in the areas affected by the Kahramanmaraş earthquake. Within the scope of the measures taken in the field of disaster and emergency management under the state of emergency, Presidential Decree No. 142 has been published. Under this decree, imported machinery and equipment to be used in earthquake projects will be exempt from customs duties.

Temporary shelter materials such as containers and prefabricated structures, building materials, machinery, motor vehicles, equipment, tools, devices, spare parts, disaster and logistics equipment can be imported by AFAD (The Disaster and Emergency Management Presidency) with the opinion of the Ministry of Trade. The imports made within this scope will be exempt from customs duties, funds, fees, and other financial obligations.

The relevant article of the decree is as follows:

All kinds of prefabricated, container, and similar temporary shelter materials, building materials, machinery (including construction machinery to be purchased for public institutions and organizations), motor vehicles, equipment, tools, devices, and spare parts to be used in national and international projects carried out for the purpose of repairing the damages caused by earthquakes in the areas considered as a general disaster zone due to the earthquakes that occurred on 2/6/2023, as well as aid materials, disaster and logistics equipment, equipment, transport vehicles and vehicles to be used in emergency and disaster activities may be imported or caused to be imported by the Disaster and Emergency Management Presidency with the appropriate opinion of the Ministry of Trade. These imported goods are exempt from customs duties and all taxes, duties, funds, fees, and other financial obligations collected with this tax.

To access the Presidential Decree on Some Measures Taken in the Field of Disaster and Emergency Management within the Scope of the State of Emergency (Decree No. 142), click here.
